title: Oxygen saturation trend review
source_review_basis: full authoritative documents inspected and archived; item-level retain/rewrite/remove/add comparisons recorded

assessment.text: Supports clinician-recorded temperature, respiratory rate, oxygen saturation, mental status, eating or hydration context, and focal chest examination findings without asserting severity or diagnosis.

source.title: Pneumonia: diagnosis and management
source.population: Children, young people, and adults with community- or hospital-acquired pneumonia, subject to recommendation-specific setting and age qualifiers.
source.setting: First contact, primary care, hospital, community treatment, reassessment, and post-discharge follow-up.
source.exclusions: Exact for selected pneumonia recovery, reassessment, observations, and post-inpatient chest-radiograph fields; international guidance requires UAE clinician review and the legacy workflow does not enforce every age or discharge qualifier.

exact_location.heading: Recommendations 1.2 and 1.6.7 plus terms — clinical observations and signs

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports clinician-recorded temperature, respiratory rate, oxygen saturation, mental status, eating or hydration context, and focal chest examination findings without asserting severity or diagnosis.

assessment.text: Supports at least annual review of smoking, breathlessness, exercise tolerance, exacerbations, pulmonary rehabilitation, complications, medicine effects, inhaler technique, referral need, FEV1 and FVC, BMI, MRC dyspnoea, oxygen saturation, cor pulmonale, nutrition, mood, and social needs.
assessment.rationale: Supports structured COPD follow-up and review for oxygen-saturation trend review; no decision is generated.

source.title: Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease in over 16s: diagnosis and management
source.population: People aged over 16 years with chronic obstructive pulmonary disease.
source.setting: Primary and specialist COPD diagnosis, monitoring, and ongoing management.
source.exclusions: Exact for adult COPD follow-up and exacerbation-feature documentation; not applicable to children and not directly validated for UAE prescribing or referral pathways.

exact_location.heading: Recommendations 1.2.137–1.2.140 and Table 6 — follow-up and review

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports at least annual review of smoking, breathlessness, exercise tolerance, exacerbations, pulmonary rehabilitation, complications, medicine effects, inhaler technique, referral need, FEV1 and FVC, BMI, MRC dyspnoea, oxygen saturation, cor pulmonale, nutrition, mood, and social needs.
